problem_structure.unknown: How to fill the order correctly when two candidate solutions both appear to match the written slip
data: Two sets of containers on the shelf, both matching the written order slip — one set matches by standard-formulation protocol, the other matches when cross-referenced against the reference chart
A written order slip on the counter beside a flat square verification panel
A reference chart pinned to the wall above the shelves showing formulary cross-references and interaction corrections
A flat square verification panel on the counter, dark and faintly warm
problem_structure.condition: The filled order must be placed on the verification panel and the panel must light green — a protocol-only fill that ignores the reference chart will not pass

correct_approach: Read the reference chart to cross-check the order slip against known interactions and corrections; select the chart-correct set rather than the protocol-correct set; place it on the verification panel; press the panel and wait for the green light before assuming closure.
